page.title: Azimut 39 vs Azimut 58: When to Upgrade in Marbella

data.meta_description: Azimut 39 vs Azimut 58 in Marbella: compare space, speed, and price for your charter. Find out when to upgrade and book your ideal boat today.
data.summary: Compare the Azimut 39 and Azimut 58 for Marbella charters. Learn which boat suits your group size, budget, and cruising style, with concrete specs and pricing.
key_facts: Azimut 39: 12.5m, up to 11 guests, departs Puerto Banús.
Azimut 58 Flybridge: 17.6m, up to 12 guests, departs Puerto Banús.
Azimut 39: €749 for 2 hours, €2,299 for 8 hours (skipper, drinks, VAT included).
Azimut 58: pricing on request via WhatsApp (no fixed hourly rate).
Azimut 39 has a top speed of ~30 knots; Azimut 58 cruises at ~26 knots.
Both boats include a licensed skipper, fuel, soft drinks, beer, wine, cava, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA.
Azimut 58 offers a flybridge, larger saloon, and more deck space for lounging.

faq.q: What is the main difference between the Azimut 39 and Azimut 58?
faq.a: The Azimut 39 is a 12.5m sport cruiser with a top speed of 30 knots, ideal for shorter trips and smaller groups. The Azimut 58 is a 17.6m flybridge yacht with more deck space, a flybridge lounge, and a larger saloon, better for all-day cruising and up to 12 guests.

faq.q: How many guests can the Azimut 39 and Azimut 58 accommodate?
faq.a: The Azimut 39 can hold up to 11 guests, while the Azimut 58 can take up to 12 guests. Both are perfect for groups, but the Azimut 58 offers more room to spread out.

faq.q: What is the price difference between the Azimut 39 and Azimut 58?
faq.a: The Azimut 39 has fixed pricing: €749 for 2 hours, €2,299 for 8 hours. The Azimut 58's price is on request via WhatsApp, as it depends on season, duration, and extras. Generally, the Azimut 58 costs more due to its size and premium features.

faq.q: Which boat is faster: Azimut 39 or Azimut 58?
faq.a: The Azimut 39 is faster, with a top speed of around 30 knots, making it a sportier choice. The Azimut 58 cruises at about 26 knots, offering a smoother, more relaxed ride.

faq.q: What is the best use case for the Azimut 39 in Marbella?
faq.a: The Azimut 39 is great for a quick sunset cruise from Puerto Banús to Cabopino or a 4-hour trip to Cala del Faro. Its speed and lower cost make it ideal for short, lively outings with friends.

faq.q: When should I upgrade to the Azimut 58?
faq.a: Upgrade to the Azimut 58 if you have a full group of 12, want a flybridge for sunbathing, or plan an all-day charter to Estepona or Sotogrande. The extra space and comfort justify the higher cost for longer trips.

faq.q: Do both boats depart from the same marina?
faq.a: Yes, both the Azimut 39 and Azimut 58 depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella's premier marina. You can easily walk from the Golden Mile or Marbella city centre.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price for both boats?
faq.a: Both charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden fees.

data.body_html:
Choosing between the Azimut 39 and the Azimut 58 for your Marbella boat charter can feel like comparing a nimble sports car to a luxury SUV. Both are exceptional Italian-built yachts from the same stable, but they serve different moods, group sizes, and budgets. In this guide, we break down the specs, pricing, and real-world use cases so you can decide which vessel is right for your day on the Costa del Sol.

Azimut 39 vs Azimut 58: Key Specs at a Glance

The Azimut 39 measures 12.5 metres and can carry up to 11 guests, while the Azimut 58 Flybridge stretches to 17.6 metres and accommodates up to 12 guests. Both depart from Puerto Banús, but the Azimut 58's flybridge and larger saloon make it a step up in comfort. The Azimut 39 is faster—around 30 knots top speed—versus the Azimut 58's cruising pace of about 26 knots. For a full comparison of our fleet, visit our [boat rental Marbella](/) page.

Pricing: Azimut 39 Has a Fixed Rate, Azimut 58 Is Bespoke

The Azimut 39 offers transparent pricing: €749 for 2 hours, €2,299 for 8 hours, with skipper, drinks, and VAT included. The Azimut 58, being a larger premium yacht, has pricing on request via WhatsApp. Expect a higher daily rate for the 58, but the added space and flybridge make it worth it for longer charters. If you're on a tighter budget, the Azimut 39 delivers excellent value for a half-day trip.

Which Boat Is Best for Your Group Size?

With 11 guests, the Azimut 39 feels sociable and intimate—perfect for a group of friends or a family outing. The Azimut 58, with 12 guests, offers more deck space, a separate flybridge lounge, and a larger saloon with air conditioning. If your party values room to roam, upgrade to the 58. For a cosy vibe, the 39 is ideal. Check out our Azimut 39 charter page for more details.

Cruising Range: Where Can You Go?

From Puerto Banús, the Azimut 39 can easily reach Cabopino (15 minutes), Cala del Faro (30 minutes), or Estepona (45 minutes). The Azimut 58, with its larger fuel capacity and comfortable cruising speed, is better suited for longer runs to Sotogrande or even Gibraltar. For a sunset trip to Cala Cortés, both boats work, but the 58's flybridge offers a superior vantage point.

Onboard Experience: Sporty vs Luxurious

The Azimut 39 is a sport cruiser with an open cockpit and a sleek profile. It's fast, responsive, and fun—ideal for water sports or a quick blast along the coast. The Azimut 58 is a flybridge yacht with a spacious interior, a full galley, and a sun pad area. It's built for relaxation and entertaining. Both include free soft drinks, beer, wine, cava, and snacks, but the 58 adds a more premium feel.

When to Upgrade from the Azimut 39 to the Azimut 58

Consider upgrading if: you're chartering for a full day (8+ hours), you have 11–12 guests, you want a flybridge for sunbathing, or you're celebrating a special occasion. The Azimut 58 also handles rougher seas better, making it a safer choice for windy days. If your trip is 2–4 hours and your group is 6–10 people, the Azimut 39 is more cost-effective. Browse our Azimut 58 Flybridge charter for full specs.

What’s Included on Both Boats?

Every charter from our fleet includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and 21% Spanish IVA. No hidden extras. The Azimut 58 may offer optional catering or a jet ski add-on (ask via WhatsApp). Both boats depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella's iconic marina.

Frequently asked questions

What is the main difference between the Azimut 39 and Azimut 58?

The Azimut 39 is a 12.5m sport cruiser with a top speed of 30 knots, ideal for shorter trips and smaller groups. The Azimut 58 is a 17.6m flybridge yacht with more deck space, a flybridge lounge, and a larger saloon, better for all-day cruising and up to 12 guests.

How many guests can the Azimut 39 and Azimut 58 accommodate?

The Azimut 39 can hold up to 11 guests, while the Azimut 58 can take up to 12 guests. Both are perfect for groups, but the Azimut 58 offers more room to spread out.

What is the price difference between the Azimut 39 and Azimut 58?

The Azimut 39 has fixed pricing: €749 for 2 hours, €2,299 for 8 hours. The Azimut 58's price is on request via WhatsApp, as it depends on season, duration, and extras. Generally, the Azimut 58 costs more due to its size and premium features.

Which boat is faster: Azimut 39 or Azimut 58?

The Azimut 39 is faster, with a top speed of around 30 knots, making it a sportier choice. The Azimut 58 cruises at about 26 knots, offering a smoother, more relaxed ride.

What is the best use case for the Azimut 39 in Marbella?

The Azimut 39 is great for a quick sunset cruise from Puerto Banús to Cabopino or a 4-hour trip to Cala del Faro. Its speed and lower cost make it ideal for short, lively outings with friends.

When should I upgrade to the Azimut 58?

Upgrade to the Azimut 58 if you have a full group of 12, want a flybridge for sunbathing, or plan an all-day charter to Estepona or Sotogrande. The extra space and comfort justify the higher cost for longer trips.

Do both boats depart from the same marina?

Yes, both the Azimut 39 and Azimut 58 depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella's premier marina. You can easily walk from the Golden Mile or Marbella city centre.

What is included in the charter price for both boats?

Both charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden fees.

Still unsure which boat fits your plans? Get in touch via WhatsApp for a personal recommendation. And don't forget to explore our full [boat rental Marbella](/) collection to see all available yachts.
